entries.summary: starting point - converted from godot project

modern lighting engine: ported renderer to WebGPU + TSL with a node-based post pipeline (GTAO ambient occlusion, screen-space reflections, full-scene bloom, SMAA), PCF soft shadows, and emissive coins. Pinned three.js to 0.185.0.

added a lil-gui debug panel for live control of lighting and post (exposure, sun/hemisphere intensity, shadow softness, GTAO, SSR, bloom); AO intensity exposed as a uniform. Replaced deprecated THREE.Clock with THREE.Timer. three.js already on latest (0.185.0).

baked hand-tuned lighting/post defaults from the GUI: exposure 1.0, sun 1.35, hemisphere 1.35, shadow softness 10; GTAO strength 0.75 / radius 0.3 / thickness 2 / samples 20; SSR intensity 0.41 / maxDistance 3 / thickness 0; bloom radius 0.6.

removed screen-space reflections (SSR) — not performant and didn't suit the matte cartoon scene. Dropped the SSRNode import, the metalness/roughness MRT attachment it required, and its GUI folder. Pipeline is now GTAO + bloom + SMAA with PCF soft shadows.

multiplayer stage 1 (real-time player presence) via Colyseus. Added server/ — a plain-ESM Colyseus 0.16 server (schema v3 to match colyseus.js client) with a 'game' room syncing each player's position/rotation/animation/name/color; binds 0.0.0.0:$PORT for Render. Client (index.html) connects via colyseus.js from esm.sh, renders color-tinted remote avatars with interpolation, throttles its own transform at 20Hz, and degrades gracefully to single-player if the server is unreachable. Verified locally with a bot client. Next: server-authoritative shared world (coins/platforms/bricks) + InstantDB leaderboard, then deploy server to Render.

deployed the Colyseus server to Render and wired the production client to it (RENDER_WS_URL = wss://platformer-server-zxo4.onrender.com). Verified the deployed matchmaking endpoint over https and a real browser->wss->Render room join end-to-end.

added a bottom name + color picker UI (8-swatch palette), persisted to localStorage. Choice tints your own avatar live and syncs to others; remote players now show floating CSS2D name tags above their heads. Server gained a 'profile' message handler for live name/color updates (needs a Render redeploy for production live-changes; initial values still flow via join options). Guarded game keys so typing your name doesn't move the character. Decided to go client-side for InstantDB (App ID only, no admin token) — leaderboard wiring is the next step; admin token can be removed from Render.

client-side InstantDB integration (@instantdb/core via esm.sh, public App ID only). Stable per-player id via instantId() in localStorage. Coin counter is now a lifetime 'totalCoins' persisted to localStorage + upserted to a 'players' entity (name/color/totalCoins) on a 600ms debounce; respawn no longer resets it. Firebase-style presence via db.joinRoom('game','lobby') + publish/subscribePresence drives an online indicator. New pop-open leaderboard panel (🏆 button) lists players sorted by coins (sorted in JS, no index needed) with online/offline dot, color swatch, name, and '(you)' highlight. Live query verified end-to-end incl. multi-row sort, presence online/offline, and live add/delete. Graceful no-op if InstantDB is unreachable.

Fall Guys-style UI restyle (playful mobile look). Global rounded font (ui-rounded / SF Pro Rounded). Name tags are now bubbly white-bordered purple pills sitting just above the head (lowered from +0.5 to +0.15). Leaderboard restyled as a white candy card with purple gradient header, 🥇🥈🥉 medals for the top 3, bordered online/offline dots, color swatches, gold coin icons, and a gold-highlighted 'you' row. Leaderboard button is a chunky 3D orange candy pill with a press-down active state. Name/color picker is a white rounded pill bar with a bouncy scaled-up selected swatch. Verified in-browser; no errors.

Reworked the playful UI to match the Fall Guys look more closely: thick dark-navy (#1c2540) outlines on every element, cyan + magenta palette, chunky navy bottom-shadows (3D press feel), uppercase white text with navy text-shadows. Name tags are now magenta pills with a navy outline and a downward pointer triangle. Leaderboard is a cyan card with a diagonally-striped header band and white navy-outlined rows ('you' row in yellow). Picker bar and swatches gained navy outlines; selected swatch keeps the bouncy scale + yellow ring. Verified in-browser; no errors.

Refined name tags to match the reference exactly: removed the navy border, smaller text (11px), and a small white arrow floating detached below the pill (gap, not connected). Raised the tag higher above the head (PLAYER.height +0.15 -> +0.35). Verified in-browser; no errors.

Player color now recolors ONLY the default-purple suit instead of emissive-tinting the whole model. The character is a single shared texture atlas (Kenney colormap), so tintCharacter now bakes a per-color copy of the atlas: detects the suit's bluish-violet purple by hue (B > R > G and B-G > 60 — catches the whole shaded gradient ~#623ebb..#a878e8, excludes face/white/skin/blue/etc.) and recolors it to the chosen color while preserving each pixel's brightness (factor = maxChannel/232) so the baked shading survives. Result cached per color (CanvasTexture, sRGB, nearest, flipY=false) and swapped onto each character instance's cloned material. Verified: full suit recolors with shading intact, face/torso/stalk unchanged; no errors.

Emoji faces: pick a unicode emoji from a picker and it renders on the model's face plate. In the baked per-player atlas the gold face cell is recolored to black (alongside the suit recolor); a small quad (PlaneGeometry, MeshBasicMaterial, toneMapped:false) parented to the 'torso' mesh at local (0,0.33,0.345) carries a canvas texture = black rounded screen + the emoji glyph (system emoji font). setFace(charObj, emoji) creates/updates the plate per character instance. New emoji picker UI (button + 16-emoji popover) in the identity bar; choice persists to localStorage (pf_emoji), syncs over Colyseus (added emoji to the Player schema + join options + profile message; server needs a Render redeploy for prod) and into the InstantDB players row. Remotes render + live-update their emoji faces. Verified local + remote in-browser; no errors.

Polished emoji plate (emoji centered via +12px baseline nudge; quad 0.4->0.36; z 0.345->0.339 to sit flush) then replaced the hardcoded 16-emoji grid with a real emoji picker library: emoji-picker-element (the <emoji-picker> web component) via esm.sh, with search / categories / skin tones. Themed to match (navy border, 14px radius, offset shadow via CSS custom props). Wired its 'emoji-click' event to the existing setFace + persist + sync; closes on outside click; keyboard guard extended so typing in its search doesn't move the player. Verified in-browser; no errors.
